narrative.headline: 2020 Honda Civic: electrical and power train lead owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 213 complaints with NHTSA. The electrical system is the most reported area at 16%, just ahead of the power train (15%) and air bags (14%). No deaths are reported. The air bags stand out on severity, drawing the most crashes (5), the most injuries (5), and the only fire among the top areas, so they are worth a close look.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 34 reports (16%), linked to 3 crashes and 3 injuries, no fires. Watch for dashboard error messages and warning lights.

issues.sample: TL* THE CONTACT OWNS A 2020 HONDA CIVIC. WHILE DRIVING APPROXIMATELY 35 MPH AND ATTEMPTING TO ACCELERATE, THE VEHICLE SURGED FORWARD. THE CONTACT STATED THAT THE FAILURE OCCURRED TWICE. THE VEHICLE WAS TAKEN TO ATLANTIC HONDA (1375 SUNRISE HWY, BAY SHORE, NY 11706) WHERE IT WAS D
issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 31 reports (15%), tied to 1 crash, with no fires or injuries. Look for surging or hesitation when accelerating.

issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: 29 reports (14%) but the most serious by outcome: 5 crashes, 5 injuries, and the only fire (1) among the top areas. Worth checking carefully.

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: 27 reports (13%), linked to 2 crashes and 4 injuries, no fires. Watch for stiffness or the wheel jerking on its own while driving.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: 24 reports (11%) with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Watch for warning lights and sudden loss of power while driving.
